sourceMovie.title: Annabelle Comes Home
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Annabelle Comes Home works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Exorcist
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Annabelle Comes Home fans keep landing on The Exorcist (1973) — the horror is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Scream
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Scream draws from the same horror well as Annabelle Comes Home, channeling crime in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1996 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Annabelle: Creation
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Annabelle: Creation (2017) captures the horror appeal of Annabelle Comes Home without simply imitating it. The dark streak running through both films is what puts Annabelle: Creation at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Nun II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror appeal of Annabelle Comes Home — that dark edge, the craft — translates cleanly into The Nun II (2023). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Countdown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Countdown (2019) clearly understood what makes horror work — the same quality that keeps Annabelle Comes Home rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Child's Play 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Child's Play 2 (1990) is the kind of film Annabelle Comes Home fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The horror overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Babysitter: Killer Queen
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Babysitter: Killer Queen shares Annabelle Comes Home's gift for horror — the 2020 film delivers comedy without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: A Haunted House 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Annabelle Comes Home compelling — the horror, the comedy — shows up in A Haunted House 2 (2014) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Cobweb
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror energy of Annabelle Comes Home is alive in Cobweb (2023), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Dolls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dolls (1986) operates in the same horror as Annabelle Comes Home, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

faq.question: What should I watch after Annabelle Comes Home?
faq.answer: Try The Exorcist, Scream, Annabelle: Creation—each shares DNA with Annabelle Comes Home while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Annabelle Comes Home?
faq.answer: Annabelle Comes Home (2019) is best described as Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Annabelle Comes Home part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Annabelle Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
